decision: Non-transient 4xx (400/401/403/404, i.e. any non-2xx except 429) fails immediately with no retry and no sleep
reasoning: Matches conventions.md GitHub 4xx=permanent-caller-error rule and user goal; retrying a 403 wastes time and never succeeds

decision: Retry wait = max(Retry-After, backoff_base*2**attempt) when a numeric Retry-After header is present, else the pure backoff formula
reasoning: Discord sends Retry-After on 429; honoring it avoids hammering while backoff still floors the wait; per user goal
Backward-compat escape hatch; total attempts = max_retries+1 makes 0 = today's behavior, keeps existing call sites safe

decision: MODIFIED: transient failures are retried before becoming fatal; non-transient 4xx stays fatal on first attempt
reasoning: Adding retry to a fatal-on-first-error adapter MODIFIES the fatal requirement (v3-llm-throttle lesson) — a pure ADD would leave two contradictory specs (fail-now vs retry-then-fail)
rejected: pure ADD leaving BR-V2-005-004 no-retry clause intact

decision: attempt index starts at 0 so waits are backoff_base*{1,2,4,...}
reasoning: Natural reading of backoff_base*2**attempt; AC pins the formula and monotonic growth, exact first-wait indexing left to dev

decision: Retry is per-POST (per split message / per embed batch); no rollback of already-delivered messages
reasoning: Preserves existing BR-V2-005-004 no-rollback multi-message semantics; each POST is independent
Explicit closed classification so dev/QA have no ambiguity on which errors retry

decision: Retry-After parsed as seconds; missing/empty/non-numeric ignored, fall back to backoff, never crash
reasoning: Untrusted-response-data convention: guard against malformed header rather than assuming shape
Retry+backoff+classify+URL-safe-error must be identical for plain-text and embed POSTs; one helper prevents drift (S2 handoff §4 risk; v2-006 lesson: parameterize the one retry helper, don't fork)

decision: Backoff = max(Retry-After, backoff_base*2**attempt) with attempt from 0; Retry-After parsed as finite float else None (never crash); sleep only between attempts
reasoning: attempt=0 makes first-retry wait=backoff_base=1.0, matching AC-001-006 max(5,1.0); untrusted-header guard per conventions; timeout/network failures have no response so use pure backoff

decision: Added `import time` and `from collections.abc import Callable` at module top; stored max_retries/backoff_base/sleep as self._* in __init__
reasoning: Matches design.md Implementation Guide; Callable from collections.abc preferred over typing.Callable per UP rules (ruff UP)

decision: _parse_retry_after implemented as @staticmethod on DiscordDelivery; uses math.isfinite guard; math imported locally
reasoning: Co-locates helper with class; avoids polluting module namespace; mirrors existing local `import re` in _parse_sections

decision: _do_post_with_retry uses while True + attempt counter from 0; TimeoutException caught before RequestError; error text from status/type(exc).__name__ only; sleep only in retry branch never after final failure
reasoning: ADR-001/ADR-002 exact spec; v2-005-push-delivery lesson: TimeoutException is subclass of RequestError — must catch first

decision: _post_one and _post_one_embed each reduced to a single _do_post_with_retry(...) call; noun/unit differ between the two
reasoning: ADR-001 single helper; zero retry-logic duplication; both callers tested for identical retry policy (AC-001-008)

decision: Split test_non_2xx_raises_delivery_error: [400,401,404] parametrize stays immediate-error; [429,500,503] moved to test_transient_exhausted_raises_delivery_error with max_retries=2 + fake sleep
reasoning: AC-001-004 non-transient=immediate; AC-001-002 transient=retry-then-error; old merged parametrize would fail because 429/500/503 no longer raise on first attempt
rejected: keep original parametrize and assert call_count >= 1

decision: GO — 0 bugs found; 17 ACs verified; 81 tests pass; 94% coverage; T1 URL leak confirmed clean in source
reasoning: All three critical invariants (URL secrecy, backoff formula, non-transient immediate fail) verified in source code and test assertions; no hollow TCs; no deviations from spec
